items.summary: In backend/main.mo, add a new public type `SubscriptionTier` with fields: `id: Text`, `name: Text`, `maxBots: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `maxApiCalls: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `priceInCents: Nat`, `features: [Text]`, `active: Bool`.

Persist subscription tiers in stable storage keyed by `id`, and maintain an in-memory lookup map keyed by `id` for efficient reads/writes. Do not remove or change any legacy public types/methods listed in the file header compatibility inventory.

Seed default tiers during initialization if the stable tier store is empty: `free` (name "Paper Hand", maxBots= ?1, maxApiCalls=?1000, priceInCents=0, active=true), `pro` (name "Diamond Hand", maxBots=?5, maxApiCalls=?50000, priceInCents=5000, active=true), `whale` (name "Whale", maxBots=null, maxApiCalls=null, priceInCents=50000, active=true).

Implement public query methods expected by the frontend: `list_tiers()` returning `Result<[SubscriptionTier]>` and `get_tier(id : Text)` returning `Result<SubscriptionTier>`. These methods must be publicly accessible (not admin-only) and must return variants matching the existing `Result` type pattern (`#ok` / `#err`) so the frontend can rely on `__kind__ === 'ok'` / `__kind__ === 'err'` decoding.

Add admin-only tier management update calls using AccessControl.isAdmin for authorization checks: `create_tier(tier : SubscriptionTier)`, `update_tier(id : Text, patch : { name : ?Text; maxBots : ??Nat; maxApiCalls : ??Nat; priceInCents : ?Nat; features : ?[Text]; active : ?Bool })` (partial update semantics), and `toggle_tier_active(id : Text, active : Bool)`. All mutations must be `shared` update calls (not query). `create_tier` must reject duplicate `id` with `#err(...)` and must not overwrite existing tiers.
